title: psychotria guadalupensis subsp. grosourdieana
intro: Psychotria guadalupensis subsp. grosourdieana, also known as Grosourdy's wild coffee, is a subspecies of the Psychotria guadalupensis plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the island of Guadeloupe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Psychotria guadalupensis subsp. grosourdieana are attributed to its rich content of alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, and can also be used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various forms of herbal preparation to harness its medicinal properties.
